export an organizational user's data.

If successful, this method returns a 202 Accepted response code.
It does not return anything in the response body. The response contains the following response headers.

:param str storage_location: This is a shared access signature (SAS) URL to an Azure Storage account,
    to where data should be exported.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.find_meeting_times
documentation:
Suggest meeting times and locations based on organizer and attendees availability, and time or location
constraints specified as parameters.

If findMeetingTimes cannot return any meeting suggestions, the response would indicate a reason in the
emptySuggestionsReason property. Based on this value, you can better adjust the parameters
and call findMeetingTimes again.

The algorithm used to suggest meeting times and locations undergoes fine-tuning from time to time.
In scenarios like test environments where the input parameters and calendar data remain static, expect
that the suggested results may differ over time.

:param list[AttendeeBase] or None attendees: A collection of attendees or resources for the meeting.
    Since findMeetingTimes assumes that any attendees who is a person is always required, specify required
    for a person and resource for a resource in the corresponding type property. An empty collection causes
    findMeetingTimes to look for free time slots for only the organizer. Optional.
:param office365.outlook.calendar.location_constraint.LocationConstraint or None location_constraint:
    The organizer's requirements about the meeting location, such as whether a suggestion for a meeting
    location is required, or there are specific locations only where the meeting can take place. Optional.
:param TimeConstraint time_constraint: Any time restrictions for a meeting, which can include the nature
    of the meeting (activityDomain property) and possible meeting time periods (timeSlots property).
    findMeetingTimes assumes activityDomain as work if you don't specify this parameter. Optional.
:param str meeting_duration: The length of the meeting, denoted in ISO8601 format.
     For example, 1 hour is denoted as 'PT1H', where 'P' is the duration designator,
     'T' is the time designator, and 'H' is the hour designator.
     Use M to indicate minutes for the duration; for example, 2 hours and 30 minutes would be 'PT2H30M'.
     If no meeting duration is specified, findMeetingTimes uses the default of 30 minutes. Optional.
:param int max_candidates: The maximum number of meeting time suggestions to be returned. Optional.
:param bool is_organizer_optional: Specify True if the organizer doesn't necessarily have to attend.
     The default is false. Optiona
:param bool return_suggestion_reasons: Specify True to return a reason for each meeting suggestion in
     the suggestionReason property. The default is false to not return that property. Optional.
:param float minimum_attendee_percentage: The minimum required confidence for a time slot to be returned
    in the response. It is a % value ranging from 0 to 100. Optional.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.get_calendar_view
documentation:
Get the occurrences, exceptions, and single instances of events in a calendar view defined by a time range,
   from the user's default calendar, or from some other calendar of the user's.

:param datetime.datetime end_dt: The end date and time of the time range, represented in ISO 8601 format.
     For example, "2019-11-08T20:00:00-08:00".
:param datetime.datetime start_dt: The start date and time of the time range, represented in ISO 8601 format.
    For example, "2019-11-08T19:00:00-08:00".

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.get_reminder_view
documentation:
Get the occurrences, exceptions, and single instances of events in a calendar view defined by a time range,
           from the user's default calendar, or from some other calendar of the user's.

:param datetime.datetime end_dt: The end date and time of the event for which the reminder is set up.
    The value is represented in ISO 8601 format, for example, "2015-11-08T20:00:00.0000000"..
:param datetime.datetime start_dt: The start date and time of the event for which the reminder is set up.
    The value is represented in ISO 8601 format, for example, "2015-11-08T19:00:00.0000000".

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.get_managed_app_diagnostic_statuses
documentation: Gets diagnostics validation status for a given user.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.get_managed_app_policies
documentation: Gets app restrictions for a given user.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.delete_object
documentation:
---
param permanent_delete: Permanently deletes the user from directory
type permanent_delete: bool
---

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.revoke_signin_sessions
documentation:
Invalidates all the refresh tokens issued to applications for a user
(as well as session cookies in a user's browser), by resetting the signInSessionsValidFromDateTime user
property to the current date-time. Typically, this operation is performed (by the user or an administrator)
if the user has a lost or stolen device. This operation prevents access to the organization's data through
applications on the device by requiring the user to sign in again to all applications that they have previously
consented to, independent of device.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.reprocess_license_assignment
documentation:
Reprocess all group-based license assignments for the user. To learn more about group-based licensing,
see What is group-based licensing in Azure Active Directory. Also see Identify and resolve license assignment
problems for a group in Azure Active Directory for more details.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.remove_all_devices_from_management
documentation: Retire all devices from management for this user

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.wipe_managed_app_registrations_by_device_tag
documentation:
Issues a wipe operation on an app registration with specified device tag.
:param str device_tag: device tag

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.translate_exchange_ids
documentation:
Translate identifiers of Outlook-related resources between formats.

:param list[str] input_ids: A collection of identifiers to convert. All identifiers in the collection MUST
    have the same source ID type, and MUST be for items in the same mailbox. Maximum size of this collection
    is 1000 strings.
:param str source_id_type: The ID type of the identifiers in the InputIds parameter.
:param str target_id_type: The requested ID type to convert to.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.created_datetime
documentation:
The Timestamp type represents date and time information using ISO 8601 format and is always in UTC time.
For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2014 is 2014-01-01T00:00:00Z

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.created_objects
documentation: Directory objects created by this user.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.sign_in_activity
documentation: Get the last signed-in date and request ID of the sign-in for a given user. Read-only.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.account_enabled
documentation: True if the account is enabled; otherwise, false. This property is required when a user is created.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.age_group
documentation: Gets the age group of the user

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.app_role_assignments
documentation: Get the apps and app roles which this user has been assigned.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.chats
documentation: The user's chats.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.given_name
documentation: The given name (first name) of the user. Maximum length is 64 characters

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.my_site
documentation: The URL for the user's site.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.office_location
documentation: The office location in the user's place of business.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.user_principal_name
documentation:
The user principal name (UPN) of the user. The UPN is an Internet-style login name for the user based on the
Internet standard RFC 822. By convention, this should map to the user's email name.
The general format is alias@domain, where domain must be present in the tenant's collection of verified domains.
This property is required when a user is created. The verified domains for the tenant can be accessed from
the verifiedDomains property of organization.
NOTE: This property cannot contain accent characters.
Only the following characters are allowed A - Z, a - z, 0 - 9, ' . - _ ! # ^ ~.
For the complete list of allowed characters, see username policies.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.assigned_plans
documentation: The plans that are assigned to the user.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.authentication
documentation: The authentication methods that are supported for the user.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.business_phones
documentation:
String collection       The telephone numbers for the user. NOTE: Although this is a string collection,
only one number can be set for this property. Read-only for users synced from on-premises directory.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.creation_type
documentation:
Indicates whether the user account was created as a regular school or work account (null),
an external account (Invitation), a local account for an Azure Active Directory B2C tenant (LocalAccount)
or self-service sign-up using email verification (EmailVerified). Read-only.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.license_assignment_states
documentation:
State of license assignments for this user. Also indicates licenses that are directly-assigned and those
that the user has inherited through group memberships.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.mail
documentation:
The SMTP address for the user, for example, "jeff@contoso.onmicrosoft.com".
Returned by default. Supports $filter and endsWith.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.other_mails
documentation:
A list of additional email addresses for the user;
for example: ["bob@contoso.com", "Robert@fabrikam.com"]. Supports $filter.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.interests
documentation: A list for the user to describe their interests.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.identities
documentation:
Represents the identities that can be used to sign in to this user account.
An identity can be provided by Microsoft (also known as a local account), by organizations,
or by social identity providers such as Facebook, Google, and Microsoft, and tied to a user account.
May contain multiple items with the same signInType value.
Supports $filter.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.activities
documentation: The user's activities across devices.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.assigned_licenses
documentation: The licenses that are assigned to the user, including inherited (group-based) licenses.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.insights
documentation: Insights are relationships calculated using advanced analytics and machine learning techniques.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.photo
documentation: The user's profile photo. Read-only.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.photos
documentation: The collection of the user's profile photos in different sizes

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.manager
documentation: The user or contact that is this user's manager

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.preferred_language
documentation: The preferred language for the user. Should follow ISO 639-1 Code; for example en-US.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.mailbox_settings
documentation: Get the user's mailboxSettings.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.calendar
documentation: The user's primary calendar. Read-only.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.calendars
documentation: The user's calendar groups. Read-only. Nullable.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.calendar_groups
documentation: The user's calendar groups. Read-only. Nullable.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.license_details
documentation: Retrieve the properties and relationships of a Drive resource.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.drive
documentation: Retrieve the properties and relationships of a Drive resource.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.contacts
documentation:
Get a contact collection from the default Contacts folder of the signed-in user (.../me/contacts),
or from the specified contact folder.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.contact_folders
documentation: Get the contact folder collection in the default Contacts folder of the signed-in user.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.events
documentation: Get an event collection or an event.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.messages
documentation: Get an event collection or an event.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.joined_teams
documentation: Get the teams in Microsoft Teams that the user is a direct member of.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.managed_devices
documentation: Devices that are managed or pre-enrolled through Intune

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.member_of
documentation: Get groups and directory roles that the user is a direct member of.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.on_premises_distinguished_name
documentation:
Contains the on-premises Active Directory distinguished name or DN. The property is only populated for
customers who are synchronizing their on-premises directory to Azure Active Directory via Azure AD Connect

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.on_premises_domain_name
documentation:
Contains the on-premises domainFQDN, also called dnsDomainName synchronized from the on-premises directory.
The property is only populated for customers who are synchronizing their on-premises directory to
Azure Active Directory via Azure AD Connect.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.owned_devices
documentation:
Devices that are owned by the user. Read-only. Nullable.
Supports $expand and $filter (/$count eq 0, /$count ne 0, /$count eq 1, /$count ne 1).

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.owned_objects
documentation: Directory objects that are owned by the user. Read-only. Nullable. Supports $expand.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.proxy_addresses
documentation:
For example: ["SMTP: bob@contoso.com", "smtp: bob@sales.contoso.com"]. Changes to the mail property will
also update this collection to include the value as an SMTP address. For more information, see mail and
proxyAddresses properties. The proxy address prefixed with SMTP (capitalized) is the primary proxy address
while those prefixed with smtp are the secondary proxy addresses. For Azure AD B2C accounts, this
property has a limit of ten unique addresses. Read-only in Microsoft Graph; you can update this property
only through the Microsoft 365 admin center. Not nullable.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.transitive_member_of
documentation:
Get groups, directory roles that the user is a member of. This API request is transitive, and will also
return all groups the user is a nested member of.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.mail_folders
documentation: Get the mail folder collection under the root folder of the signed-in user.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.outlook
documentation: Represents the Outlook services available to a user.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.onenote
documentation: Represents the Onenote services available to a user.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.settings
documentation: Represents the user and organization settings object.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.planner
documentation: The plannerUser resource provide access to Planner resources for a user.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.extensions
documentation: The collection of open extensions defined for the user. Nullable.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.direct_reports
documentation: Get a user's direct reports

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.online_meetings
documentation: Get a user's online meetings.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.password_policies
documentation:
Specifies password policies for the user. This value is an enumeration with one possible value being
DisableStrongPassword, which allows weaker passwords than the default policy to be specified.
DisablePasswordExpiration can also be specified. The two may be specified together; for example:
DisablePasswordExpiration, DisableStrongPassword.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.password_profile
documentation:
Specifies the password profile for the user. The profile contains the user's password.
This property is required when a user is created. The password in the profile must satisfy minimum
requirements as specified by the passwordPolicies property. By default, a strong password is required.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.presence
documentation: Get a user's presence information.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.registered_devices
documentation: Get the devices that are registered for the user from the registeredDevices navigation property.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.street_address
documentation: The street address of the user's place of business. Maximum length is 1024 characters.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.security_identifier
documentation: Security identifier (SID) of the user, used in Windows scenarios.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.teamwork
documentation: A container for the range of Microsoft Teams functionalities that are available per user in the tenant.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.todo
documentation: Represents the To Do services available to a user.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.employee_experience
documentation: Represents the To Do services available to a user.

methods.name: office365.directory.users.user.User.usage_location
documentation:
A two letter country code (ISO standard 3166). Required for users that will be assigned licenses due to
legal requirement to check for availability of services in countries. Examples include: US, JP, and GB.
Represents an Azure AD user account. Inherits from directoryObject.

classes.name: office365.directory.audit.provisioning.service_principal.ProvisioningServicePrincipal
documentation: Represents the service principal used for provisioning.

classes.name: office365.directory.audit.directory.DirectoryAudit

methods.name: office365.directory.audit.directory.DirectoryAudit.activity_datetime
documentation: Indicates the date and time the activity was performed.

methods.name: office365.directory.audit.directory.DirectoryAudit.activity_display_name
documentation:
Indicates the activity name or the operation name (examples: "Create User" and "Add member to group").
For a list of activities logged, refer to Azure AD audit log categories and activities.

methods.name: office365.directory.audit.directory.DirectoryAudit.category
documentation:
Indicates which resource category that's targeted by the activity.
For example: UserManagement, GroupManagement, ApplicationManagement, RoleManagement.

methods.name: office365.directory.audit.directory.DirectoryAudit.correlation_id
documentation:
Indicates a unique ID that helps correlate activities that span across various services.
Can be used to trace logs across services.

methods.name: office365.directory.audit.directory.DirectoryAudit.initiated_by
documentation: Indicates information about the user or app initiated the activity.

methods.name: office365.directory.audit.directory.DirectoryAudit.operation_type
documentation:
Indicates the type of operation that was performed. The possible values include but are not limited
to the following: Add, Assign, Update, Unassign, and Delete.

methods.name: office365.directory.audit.directory.DirectoryAudit.logged_by_service
documentation:
Indicates information on which service initiated the activity. For example:
Self-service Password Management, Core Directory, B2C, Invited Users, Microsoft Identity Manager,
Privileged Identity Management.

methods.name: office365.directory.audit.directory.DirectoryAudit.result_reason
documentation: Indicates the reason for failure if the result is failure or timeout.
Represents the directory audit items and its collection.

classes.name: office365.directory.audit.provisioning.object_summary.ProvisioningObjectSummary

methods.name: office365.directory.audit.provisioning.object_summary.ProvisioningObjectSummary.activity_datetime
documentation: Represents date and time information using ISO 8601 format and is always in UTC time.

methods.name: office365.directory.audit.provisioning.object_summary.ProvisioningObjectSummary.change_id
documentation: Unique ID of this change in this cycle. Supports $filter (eq, contains).

methods.name: office365.directory.audit.provisioning.object_summary.ProvisioningObjectSummary.cycle_id
documentation: Unique ID per job iteration. Supports $filter (eq, contains).

methods.name: office365.directory.audit.provisioning.object_summary.ProvisioningObjectSummary.duration_in_milliseconds
documentation: Indicates how long this provisioning action took to finish. Measured in milliseconds.

methods.name: office365.directory.audit.provisioning.object_summary.ProvisioningObjectSummary.service_principal
documentation: Represents the service principal used for provisioning.
Represents an action performed by the Azure AD Provisioning service and its associated properties.

classes.name: office365.directory.audit.signins.location.SignInLocation

methods.name: office365.directory.audit.signins.location.SignInLocation.__init__
documentation:
:param str city: Provides the city where the sign-in originated. This is calculated using latitude/longitude
    information from the sign-in activity.
:param str country_or_region: Provides the country code info (2 letter code) where the sign-in originated.
    This is calculated using latitude/longitude information from the sign-in activity.
:param GeoCoordinates geo_coordinates: Provides the latitude, longitude and altitude where the sign-in
    originated.
:param str state: Provides the State where the sign-in originated. This is calculated using latitude/longitude
    information from the sign-in activity.
Provides the city, state and country/region from where the sign-in happened.

classes.name: office365.directory.audit.log_root.AuditLogRoot

methods.name: office365.directory.audit.log_root.AuditLogRoot.directory_audits
documentation:
Get the list of audit logs generated by Azure Active Directory. This includes audit logs generated
by various services within Azure AD, including user, app, device and group Management,
privileged identity management (PIM), access reviews, terms of use, identity protection,
password management (self-service and admin password resets), and self- service group management, and so on.

methods.name: office365.directory.audit.log_root.AuditLogRoot.signins
documentation:
Retrieve the Azure AD user sign-ins for your tenant. Sign-ins that are interactive in nature
(where a username/password is passed as part of auth token) and successful federated sign-ins are currently
included in the sign-in logs. The maximum and default page size is 1,000 objects and by default,
the most recent sign-ins are returned first.

methods.name: office365.directory.audit.log_root.AuditLogRoot.provisioning
documentation:
Get all provisioning events that occurred in your tenant, such as the deletion of a group in
a target application or the creation of a user when provisioning user accounts from your HR system.

Contains different types of audit logs. This resources returns a singleton auditLog resource.
It doesn't contain any usable properties.

classes.name: office365.directory.audit.signins.status.SignInStatus

methods.name: office365.directory.audit.signins.status.SignInStatus.__init__
documentation:
:param str additional_details: Provides additional details on the sign-in activity
:param int error_code: Provides the 5-6 digit error code that's generated during a sign-in failure.
:param str failure_reason: Provides the error message or the reason for failure for
    the corresponding sign-in activity.
Provides the sign-in status (Success or Failure) of the sign-in.

classes.name: office365.directory.audit.signins.activity.SignInActivity

methods.name: office365.directory.audit.signins.activity.SignInActivity.__init__
documentation:
:param datetime.datetime last_non_interactive_sign_in_datetime: The last non-interactive sign-in date for a
    specific user. You can use this field to calculate the last time a client attempted to sign into the
    directory on behalf of a user. Because some users may use clients to access tenant resources rather
    than signing into your tenant directly, you can use the non-interactive sign-in date to along with
    lastSignInDateTime to identify inactive users.
:param str last_non_interactive_sign_in_request_id: Request identifier of the last non-interactive sign-in
    performed by this user.
:param datetime.datetime last_sign_in_datetime: The last interactive sign-in date and time for a specific user.
    You can use this field to calculate the last time a user attempted to sign into the directory with an
    interactive authentication method. This field can be used to build reports, such as inactive users.
    The timestamp represents date and time information using ISO 8601 format and is always in UTC time.
    For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2014 is: '2014-01-01T00:00:00Z'. Azure AD maintains interactive
    sign-ins going back to April 2020. For more information about using the value of this property,
    see Manage inactive user accounts in Azure AD.
:param str last_sign_in_request_id: Request identifier of the last interactive sign-in performed by this user.

Provides the last interactive or non-interactive sign-in time for a specific user. Since signInActivity
describes a property of the user object, Azure AD stores sign in activity for your users for as long as the
user object exists.

classes.name: office365.directory.internal.paths.me.MePath
documentation: SignedIn user resource path

classes.name: office365.directory.synchronization.job_restart_criteria.SynchronizationJobRestartCriteria
documentation: Defines the scope of the synchronizationJob: restart action.

classes.name: office365.directory.tenant_information.TenantInformation

methods.name: office365.directory.tenant_information.TenantInformation.__str__
documentation:
:param str default_domain_name: Primary domain name of an Azure AD tenant.
:param str display_name: Display name of an Azure AD tenant.
:param str federation_brand_name: Name shown to users that sign in to an Azure AD tenant.
:param str tenant_id: Unique identifier of an Azure AD tenant.
Information about your Azure AD tenant that is publicly displayed to users in other Azure AD tenants.

classes.name: office365.directory.audit.signins.signin.SignIn

methods.name: office365.directory.audit.signins.signin.SignIn.app_display_name
documentation: App name displayed in the Azure Portal.

methods.name: office365.directory.audit.signins.signin.SignIn.app_id
documentation: Unique GUID representing the app ID in the Azure Active Directory.

methods.name: office365.directory.audit.signins.signin.SignIn.client_app_used
documentation:
Identifies the client used for the sign-in activity. Modern authentication clients include Browser, modern
clients. Legacy authentication clients include Exchange ActiveSync, IMAP, MAPI, SMTP, POP, and other clients.

methods.name: office365.directory.audit.signins.signin.SignIn.correlation_id
documentation: The request ID sent from the client when the sign-in is initiated; used to troubleshoot sign-in activity.

methods.name: office365.directory.audit.signins.signin.SignIn.created_datetime
documentation: Date and time (UTC) the sign-in was initiated.

methods.name: office365.directory.audit.signins.signin.SignIn.device_detail
documentation:
Device information from where the sign-in occurred; includes device ID, operating system, and browser.
Supports $filter (eq and startsWith operators only) on browser and operatingSytem properties.

methods.name: office365.directory.audit.signins.signin.SignIn.ip_address
documentation: IP address of the client used to sign in.

methods.name: office365.directory.audit.signins.signin.SignIn.is_interactive
documentation: Indicates if a sign-in is interactive or not.

methods.name: office365.directory.audit.signins.signin.SignIn.location
documentation:
Provides the city, state, and country code where the sign-in originated.
Supports $filter (eq and startsWith operators only) on city, state, and countryOrRegion properties.

methods.name: office365.directory.audit.signins.signin.SignIn.resource_display_name
documentation: Name of the resource the user signed into.

methods.name: office365.directory.audit.signins.signin.SignIn.resource_id
documentation: ID of the resource that the user signed into.

methods.name: office365.directory.audit.signins.signin.SignIn.risk_detail
documentation: Provides the 'reason' behind a specific state of a risky user, sign-in or a risk event.

methods.name: office365.directory.audit.signins.signin.SignIn.user_id
documentation: ID of the user that initiated the sign-in. Supports $filter (eq operator only).

methods.name: office365.directory.audit.signins.signin.SignIn.user_principal_name
documentation: User principal name of the user that initiated the sign-in. Supports $filter (eq and startsWith operators only).

methods.name: office365.directory.audit.signins.signin.SignIn.status
documentation:
Sign-in status. Includes the error code and description of the error (in case of a sign-in failure).
Supports $filter (eq operator only) on errorCode property.

Details user and application sign-in activity for a tenant (directory).
You must have an Azure AD Premium P1 or P2 license to download sign-in logs using the Microsoft Graph API.

classes.name: office365.directory.synchronization.task_execution.SynchronizationTaskExecution
documentation: Summarizes the results of the synchronization job run.

classes.name: office365.directory.synchronization.progress.SynchronizationProgress
documentation: Represents the progress of a synchronizationJob toward completion.

classes.name: office365.directory.administrative_unit.AdministrativeUnit

methods.name: office365.directory.administrative_unit.AdministrativeUnit.display_name
documentation: Display name for the administrative unit

methods.name: office365.directory.administrative_unit.AdministrativeUnit.visibility
documentation:
Controls whether the administrative unit and its members are hidden or public. Can be set to HiddenMembership.
If not set (value is null), the default behavior is public. When set to HiddenMembership, only members of
the administrative unit can list other members of the administrative unit.

methods.name: office365.directory.administrative_unit.AdministrativeUnit.members
documentation: Users and groups that are members of this administrative unit

methods.name: office365.directory.administrative_unit.AdministrativeUnit.extensions
documentation: The collection of open extensions defined for this administrative unit.

An administrative unit provides a conceptual container for user, group, and device directory objects.
Using administrative units, a company administrator can now delegate administrative responsibilities to manage
the users, groups, and devices contained within or scoped to an administrative unit to a regional or
departmental administrator. This resource is an open type that allows other properties to be passed in.

classes.name: office365.directory.synchronization.directory_definition.DirectoryDefinition
documentation:
Provides the synchronization engine information about a directory and its objects. This resource tells the
synchronization engine, for example, that the directory has objects named user and group, which attributes are
supported for those objects, and the types for those attributes. In order for the object and attribute to
participate in synchronization rules and object mappings, they must be defined as part of the directory definition.

Directory definitions are updated as part of the synchronization schema.

classes.name: office365.directory.synchronization.schema.SynchronizationSchema

methods.name: office365.directory.synchronization.schema.SynchronizationSchema.directories
documentation: Contains the collection of directories and all of their objects.

Defines what objects will be synchronized and how they will be synchronized. The synchronization schema contains
most of the setup information for a particular synchronization job. Typically, you will customize some of the
attribute mappings, or add a scoping filter to synchronize only objects that satisfy a certain condition.

The following sections describe the high-level components of the synchronization schema.

classes.name: office365.directory.synchronization.template.SynchronizationTemplate

methods.name: office365.directory.synchronization.template.SynchronizationTemplate.schema
documentation: Default synchronization schema for the jobs based on this template.

Provides pre-configured synchronization settings for a particular application. These settings will be used by
default for any synchronization job that is based on the template. The application developer specifies the template;
anyone can retrieve the template to see the default settings, including the synchronization schema.

You can provide multiple templates for an application, and designate a default template. If multiple templates
are available for the application you're interested in, seek application-specific guidance to determine which one
best meets your needs.

classes.name: office365.directory.synchronization.synchronization.Synchronization

methods.name: office365.directory.synchronization.synchronization.Synchronization.jobs
documentation:
Performs synchronization by periodically running in the background, polling for changes in one directory,
and pushing them to another directory.

methods.name: office365.directory.synchronization.synchronization.Synchronization.templates
documentation:
Performs synchronization by periodically running in the background, polling for changes in one directory,
and pushing them to another directory.

Represents the capability for Azure Active Directory (Azure AD) identity synchronization through
the Microsoft Graph API. Identity synchronization (also called provisioning) allows you to automate
the provisioning (creation, maintenance) and de-provisioning (removal) of user identities and roles from
Azure AD to supported cloud applications. For more information, see How Application Provisioning works
in Azure Active Directory

classes.name: office365.directory.synchronization.quarantine.SynchronizationQuarantine

methods.name: office365.directory.synchronization.quarantine.SynchronizationQuarantine.__init__
documentation:
:param SynchronizationError error: Describes the error(s) that occurred when putting the synchronization job
    into quarantine.
Provides information about the quarantine state of a synchronizationJob.

classes.name: office365.directory.synchronization.job.SynchronizationJob

methods.name: office365.directory.synchronization.job.SynchronizationJob.pause
documentation:
Temporarily stop a running synchronization job. All the progress, including job state, is persisted, and the
job will continue from where it left off when a start call is made.

methods.name: office365.directory.synchronization.job.SynchronizationJob.start
documentation:
Start an existing synchronization job. If the job is in a paused state, it will continue processing changes
from the point where it was paused. If the job is in quarantine, the quarantine status will be cleared.
Do not create scripts to call the start job continuously while it's running because that can cause the service
to stop running. Use the start job only when the job is currently paused or in quarantine.

methods.name: office365.directory.synchronization.job.SynchronizationJob.status
documentation: Status of the job, which includes when the job was last run, current job state, and errors.

methods.name: office365.directory.synchronization.job.SynchronizationJob.schema
documentation: The synchronization schema configured for the job.

Performs synchronization by periodically running in the background, polling for changes in one directory,
and pushing them to another directory. The synchronization job is always specific to a particular instance
of an application in your tenant. As part of the synchronization job setup, you need to give authorization
to read and write objects in your target directory, and customize the job's synchronization schema.

classes.name: office365.directory.password_credential.PasswordCredential

methods.name: office365.directory.password_credential.PasswordCredential.__init__
documentation:
:param str display_name: Friendly name for the password. Optional.
:param str secret_text: Read-only; Contains the strong passwords generated by Azure AD that are 16-64
    characters in length. The generated password value is only returned during the initial POST request to
    addPassword. There is no way to retrieve this password in the future.
:param str key_id: The unique identifier for the password.
:param str start_datetime: The date and time at which the password becomes valid. The Timestamp type represents
     date and time information using ISO 8601 format and is always in UTC time.
     For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2014 is 2014-01-01T00:00:00Z. Optional.
:param str end_datetime:

Represents a password credential associated with an application or a service principal.
The passwordCredentials property of the application entity is a collection of passwordCredential objects.

classes.name: office365.directory.synchronization.status.SynchronizationStatus

methods.name: office365.directory.synchronization.status.SynchronizationStatus.__init__
documentation:
:param list[SynchronizationProgress] progress: Details of the progress of a job toward completion.
:param SynchronizationQuarantine quarantine:
Represents the current status of the synchronizationJob.

classes.name: office365.directory.licenses.service_plan_info.ServicePlanInfo

methods.name: office365.directory.licenses.service_plan_info.ServicePlanInfo.__init__
documentation:
:param str applies_to: The object the service plan can be assigned to. Possible values:
       "User" - service plan can be assigned to individual users.
       "Company" - service plan can be assigned to the entire tenant.
:param str provisioning_status: The provisioning status of the service plan. Possible values:
       "Success" - Service is fully provisioned.
       "Disabled" - Service has been disabled.
       "PendingInput" - Service is not yet provisioned; awaiting service confirmation.
       "PendingActivation" - Service is provisioned but requires explicit activation by administrator
       (for example, Intune_O365 service plan)
       "PendingProvisioning" - Microsoft has added a new service to the product SKU and it has not been
       activated in the tenant, yet.
:param str name: The name of the service plan.
:param str _id: The unique identifier of the service plan.

Contains information about a service plan associated with a subscribed SKU. The servicePlans property of
the subscribedSku entity is a collection of servicePlanInfo.

classes.name: office365.directory.licenses.assigned_plan.AssignedPlan

methods.name: office365.directory.licenses.assigned_plan.AssignedPlan.__init__
documentation:
:param datetime.datetime assigned_datetime: The date and time at which the plan was assigned.
:param str capability_status: Condition of the capability assignment.
    The possible values are Enabled, Warning, Suspended, Deleted, LockedOut.
    See a detailed description of each value.
:param str service: The name of the service; for example, exchange.
:param str service_plan_id: A GUID that identifies the service plan. For a complete list of GUIDs and their
    equivalent friendly service names, see Product names and service plan identifiers for licensing.
The assignedPlans property of both the user entity and the organization entity is a collection of assignedPlan.

methods.name: office365.directory.licenses.units_detail.LicenseUnitsDetail.__init__
documentation: :param int enabled: The number of units that are enabled for the active subscription of the service SKU.

classes.name: office365.directory.licenses.assigned_license.AssignedLicense

methods.name: office365.directory.licenses.assigned_license.AssignedLicense.__init__
documentation:
:param str sku_id: The unique identifier for the SKU.
:param list[str] disabled_plans: A collection of the unique identifiers for plans that have been disabled.

Represents a license assigned to a user. The assignedLicenses property of the user entity is a collection
of assignedLicense.

classes.name: office365.directory.licenses.details.LicenseDetails

methods.name: office365.directory.licenses.details.LicenseDetails.service_plans
documentation: Information about the service plans assigned with the license. Read-only, Not nullable

methods.name: office365.directory.licenses.details.LicenseDetails.sku_id
documentation: Unique identifier (GUID) for the service SKU. Equal to the skuId property on the related SubscribedSku object.

methods.name: office365.directory.licenses.details.LicenseDetails.sku_part_number
documentation:
Unique SKU display name. Equal to the skuPartNumber on the related SubscribedSku object;
for example: "AAD_Premium". Read-only
Contains information about a license assigned to a user.

classes.name: office365.directory.licenses.subscribed_sku.SubscribedSku

methods.name: office365.directory.licenses.subscribed_sku.SubscribedSku.account_id
documentation: The unique ID of the account this SKU belongs to.

methods.name: office365.directory.licenses.subscribed_sku.SubscribedSku.applies_to
documentation:
The target class for this SKU. Only SKUs with target class User are assignable.
Possible values are: "User", "Company".

methods.name: office365.directory.licenses.subscribed_sku.SubscribedSku.sku_id
documentation: The unique identifier (GUID) for the service SKU.

methods.name: office365.directory.licenses.subscribed_sku.SubscribedSku.sku_part_number
documentation:
The SKU part number; for example: "AAD_PREMIUM" or "RMSBASIC".
To get a list of commercial subscriptions that an organization has acquired, see List subscribedSkus.

methods.name: office365.directory.licenses.subscribed_sku.SubscribedSku.prepaid_units
documentation: Information about the number and status of prepaid licenses.

methods.name: office365.directory.licenses.subscribed_sku.SubscribedSku.service_plans
documentation: Information about the service plans that are available with the SKU. Not nullable
Contains information about a service SKU that a company is subscribed to.

classes.name: office365.directory.licenses.assignment_state.LicenseAssignmentState

methods.name: office365.directory.licenses.assignment_state.LicenseAssignmentState.__init__
documentation:
:param str assigned_by_group: ndicates whether the license is directly assigned or inherited from a group.
     If directly assigned, this field is null; if inherited through a group membership, this field contains
     the ID of the group.
:param list[str] disabled_plans: The service plans that are disabled in this assignment
:param str error: License assignment failure error. If the license is assigned successfully, this field is Null.
     The possible values are CountViolation, MutuallyExclusiveViolation, DependencyViolation,
     ProhibitedInUsageLocationViolation, UniquenessViolation, and Other.
     For more information on how to identify and resolve license assignment errors, see here.
:param str skuId: The unique identifier for the SKU. Read-Only.
:param str state: Indicate the current state of this assignment. Read-Only.
     The possible values are Active, ActiveWithError, Disabled, and Error.

The licenseAssignmentStates property of the user entity is a collection of licenseAssignmentState objects.
It provides details about license assignments to a user. The details include information such as:

    - What plans are disabled for a user
    - Whether the license was assigned to the user directly or inherited from a group
    - The current state of the assignment
    - Error details if the assignment state is Error

classes.name: office365.directory.protection.riskyusers.risky_user.RiskyUser

methods.name: office365.directory.protection.riskyusers.risky_user.RiskyUser.history
documentation: The activity related to user risk level change

methods.name: office365.directory.protection.riskyusers.risky_user.RiskyUser.user_principal_name
documentation: Risky user principal name.

Represents Azure AD users who are at risk. Azure AD continually evaluates user risk based on various
signals and machine learning. This API provides programmatic access to all at-risk users in your Azure AD.

classes.name: office365.directory.protection.riskyusers.collection.RiskyUserCollection

methods.name: office365.directory.protection.riskyusers.collection.RiskyUserCollection.confirm_compromised
documentation:
Confirm one or more riskyUser objects as compromised. This action sets the targeted user's risk level
to high.

:param list[str] user_ids: Specify the risky user IDs to dismiss in the request body.

methods.name: office365.directory.protection.riskyusers.collection.RiskyUserCollection.dismiss
documentation:
Dismiss the risk of one or more riskyUser objects. This action sets the targeted user's risk level to none.

:param list[str] user_ids: Specify the risky user IDs to dismiss in the request body.
RiskyUser's collection

classes.name: office365.directory.protection.riskyusers.activity.RiskUserActivity
documentation: Represents the risk activites of an Azure AD user as determined by Azure AD Identity Protection.

classes.name: office365.directory.protection.riskyusers.history_item.RiskyUserHistoryItem

documentation: The activity related to user risk level change.

methods.name: office365.directory.protection.riskyusers.history_item.RiskyUserHistoryItem.initiated_by
documentation: The ID of actor that does the operation.

Represents the risk history of an Azure Active Directory (Azure AD) user as determined
by Azure AD Identity Protection.

classes.name: office365.directory.protection.risk_detection.RiskDetection
documentation:
Represents information about a detected risk in an Azure AD tenant.

Azure AD continually evaluates user risks and app or user sign-in risks based on various signals
and machine learning.
This API provides programmatic access to all risk detections in your Azure AD environment.

classes.name: office365.directory.protection.threatassessment.request.ThreatAssessmentRequest
documentation: An abstract resource type used to represent a threat assessment request item.

classes.name: office365.directory.protection.threatassessment.mail_request.MailAssessmentRequest
documentation:
Used to create and retrieve a mail threat assessment, derived from threatAssessmentRequest.

When you create a mail threat assessment request, the mail should be received by the user specified
in recipientEmail. Delegated Mail permissions (Mail.Read or Mail.Read.Shared) are requried to access the mail
received by the user or shared by someone else.

classes.name: office365.directory.protection.policy.one_drive_for_business.OneDriveForBusinessProtectionPolicy
documentation:
Contains details about protection policies applied to Microsoft 365 data in an organization.
Protection policies are defined by the Global Admin (or the SharePoint Online Admin or Exchange Online Admin)
and include what data to protect, when to protect it, and for what time period to retain the protected data
for a single Microsoft 365 service.

classes.name: office365.directory.protection.policy.base.ProtectionPolicyBase

methods.name: office365.directory.protection.policy.base.ProtectionPolicyBase.created_by
documentation: Identity of the user, device, or application which created the item.

Contains details about protection policies applied to Microsoft 365 data in an organization.
Protection policies are defined by the Global Admin (or the SharePoint Online Admin or Exchange Online Admin)
and include what data to protect, when to protect it, and for what time period to retain the protected data
for a single Microsoft 365 service.

classes.name: office365.directory.protection.root.IdentityProtectionRoot

methods.name: office365.directory.protection.root.IdentityProtectionRoot.risk_detections
documentation: Risk detection in Azure AD Identity Protection and the associated information about the detection.

methods.name: office365.directory.protection.root.IdentityProtectionRoot.risky_users
documentation: Get the teams in Microsoft Teams that the user is a direct member of.
Container for the navigation properties for Microsoft Graph identity protection resources.

classes.name: office365.directory.permissions.email_identity.EmailIdentity

methods.name: office365.directory.permissions.email_identity.EmailIdentity.__init__
documentation: :param str email:
Represents the email identity of a user.

classes.name: office365.directory.permissions.default_user_role.DefaultUserRolePermissions
documentation: Contains certain customizable permissions of default user role in Microsoft Entra ID.

classes.name: office365.directory.permissions.identity_set.IdentitySet

methods.name: office365.directory.permissions.identity_set.IdentitySet.__init__
documentation:
:param Identity application: The application associated with this action.
:param Identity device: The device associated with this action.
:param Identity user: The user associated with this action.

The IdentitySet resource is a keyed collection of identity resources. It is used to represent a set of
identities associated with various events for an item, such as created by or last modified by.

classes.name: office365.directory.protection.information.InformationProtection

methods.name: office365.directory.protection.information.InformationProtection.create_mail_assessment
documentation:
Create a mail assessment request
:param str recipient: Recipient email
:param office365.outlook.mail.messages.message.Message message: Message object or identifier
:param str expected_assessment:
:param str category:
Exposes methods that you can use to get Microsoft Purview Information Protection labels and label policies.

classes.name: office365.directory.permissions.identity.Identity

methods.name: office365.directory.permissions.identity.Identity.__init__
documentation:
:param str display_name: The display name of the identity. Note that this might not always be available or up
    to date. For example, if a user changes their display name, the API might show the new value in a future
    response, but the items associated with the user won't show up as having changed when using delta.

:param str _id: Unique identifier for the identity.

The Identity resource represents an identity of an actor. For example, an actor can be a user, device,
or application.

classes.name: office365.directory.permissions.grants.resource_specific.ResourceSpecificPermissionGrant

methods.name: office365.directory.permissions.grants.resource_specific.ResourceSpecificPermissionGrant.client_id
documentation: ID of the Azure AD app that has been granted access.

methods.name: office365.directory.permissions.grants.resource_specific.ResourceSpecificPermissionGrant.client_app_id
documentation: ID of the service principal of the Azure AD app that has been granted access.

methods.name: office365.directory.permissions.grants.resource_specific.ResourceSpecificPermissionGrant.permission
documentation: The name of the resource-specific permission.

methods.name: office365.directory.permissions.grants.resource_specific.ResourceSpecificPermissionGrant.permission_type
documentation: The type of permission. Possible values are: Application, Delegated. Read-only.

methods.name: office365.directory.permissions.grants.resource_specific.ResourceSpecificPermissionGrant.resource_app_id
documentation: ID of the Azure AD app that is hosting the resource. Read-only.

Declares the permission that has been granted to a specific Azure AD app for an instance of a resource
in Microsoft Graph.

classes.name: office365.directory.permissions.grants.condition_set.PermissionGrantConditionSet

methods.name: office365.directory.permissions.grants.condition_set.PermissionGrantConditionSet.client_application_ids
documentation:
A list of appId values for the client applications to match with, or a list with the single value all to
match any client application. Default is the single value all.

methods.name: office365.directory.permissions.grants.condition_set.PermissionGrantConditionSet.client_application_publisher_ids
documentation:
A list of Microsoft Partner Network (MPN) IDs for verified publishers of the client application,  or a list
with the single value all to match with client apps from any publisher. Default is the single value all.

methods.name: office365.directory.permissions.grants.condition_set.PermissionGrantConditionSet.client_applications_from_verified_publisher_only
documentation:
Set to true to only match on client applications with a verified publisher. Set to false to match on any client
app, even if it does not have a verified publisher. Default is false.

methods.name: office365.directory.permissions.grants.condition_set.PermissionGrantConditionSet.permissions
documentation:
The list of id values for the specific permissions to match with, or a list with the single value all to
match with any permission. The id of delegated permissions can be found in the oauth2PermissionScopes property
of the API's servicePrincipal object. The id of application permissions can be found in the appRoles property
of the API's servicePrincipal object. The id of resource-specific application permissions can be found in
the resourceSpecificApplicationPermissions property of the API's servicePrincipal object.
Default is the single value all.

methods.name: office365.directory.permissions.grants.condition_set.PermissionGrantConditionSet.resource_application
documentation:
The appId of the resource application (e.g. the API) for which a permission is being granted, or any to match
with any resource application or API. Default is any.

A permission grant condition set is used to specify a matching rule in a permission grant policy to include
or exclude a permission grant event.

A permission grant condition set contains several conditions. For an event to match a permission grant condition
set, all conditions must be met.

classes.name: office365.directory.permissions.grants.oauth2.OAuth2PermissionGrant

methods.name: office365.directory.permissions.grants.oauth2.OAuth2PermissionGrant.client_id
documentation:
The id of the client service principal for the application which is authorized to act on behalf of a signed-in
user when accessing an API. Required. Supports $filter (eq only).

methods.name: office365.directory.permissions.grants.oauth2.OAuth2PermissionGrant.consent_type
documentation:
Indicates if authorization is granted for the client application to impersonate all users or only a
specific user. AllPrincipals indicates authorization to impersonate all users.
Principal indicates authorization to impersonate a specific user. Consent on behalf of all users can be granted
by an administrator. Non-admin users may be authorized to consent on behalf of themselves in some cases,
for some delegated permissions. Required. Supports $filter (eq only).

methods.name: office365.directory.permissions.grants.oauth2.OAuth2PermissionGrant.principal_id
documentation:
The id of the user on behalf of whom the client is authorized to access the resource, when consentType is
Principal. If consentType is AllPrincipals this value is null. Required when consentType is Principal.
Supports $filter (eq only).

methods.name: office365.directory.permissions.grants.oauth2.OAuth2PermissionGrant.resource_id
documentation:
The id of the resource service principal to which access is authorized. This identifies the API which the
client is authorized to attempt to call on behalf of a signed-in user. Supports $filter (eq only).

methods.name: office365.directory.permissions.grants.oauth2.OAuth2PermissionGrant.scope
documentation:
A space-separated list of the claim values for delegated permissions which should be included in access tokens
for the resource application (the API). For example, openid User.Read GroupMember.Read.All. Each claim value
should match the value field of one of the delegated permissions defined by the API, listed in the
oauth2PermissionScopes property of the resource service principal. Must not exceed 3850 characters in length.

Represents the delegated permissions that have been granted to an application's service principal.

Delegated permissions grants can be created as a result of a user consenting the an application's request
to access an API, or created directly.

Delegated permissions are sometimes referred to as "OAuth 2.0 scopes" or "scopes".

classes.name: office365.directory.certificates.auth_configuration.CertificateBasedAuthConfiguration

methods.name: office365.directory.certificates.auth_configuration.CertificateBasedAuthConfiguration.certificate_authorities
documentation: Collection of certificate authorities which creates a trusted certificate chain.

Certificate-based authentication enables you to be authenticated by Azure Active Directory with a client certificate
on a Windows, Android, or iOS device when connecting your Exchange Online account to:

   - Microsoft mobile applications such as Outlook and Word
   - Exchange ActiveSync (EAS) clients

Configuring this feature eliminates the need to enter a username and password combination into certain mail and
Microsoft Office applications on your mobile device.
Certificate-based authentication configuration is provided through a collection of certificate authorities.
The certificate authorities are used to establish a trusted certificate chain which enables clients to
be authenticated by Azure Active Directory with a client certificate.

classes.name: office365.directory.certificates.pkcs12_information.Pkcs12CertificateInformation

methods.name: office365.directory.certificates.pkcs12_information.Pkcs12CertificateInformation.__init__
documentation:
:param str thumbprint: The certificate thumbprint
:param long not_after: The certificate's expiry. This value is a NumericDate as defined in RFC 7519
   (A JSON numeric value representing the number of seconds from 1970-01-01T00:00:00Z UTC until the specified
   UTC date/time, ignoring leap seconds.)
:param long not_before: The certificate's issue time (not before).
    This value is a NumericDate as defined in RFC 7519 (A JSON numeric value representing the number
    of seconds from 1970-01-01T00:00:00Z UTC until the specified UTC date/time, ignoring leap seconds.)
Represents the public information of a Pkcs12 certificate.

classes.name: office365.directory.permissions.scope.PermissionScope

methods.name: office365.directory.permissions.scope.PermissionScope.__init__
documentation:
:param str admin_consent_display_name: The permission's title, intended to be read by an administrator granting
    the permission on behalf of all users.
:param str admin_consent_description: A description of the delegated permissions, intended to be read
    by an administrator granting the permission on behalf of all users. This text appears in tenant-wide
    admin consent experiences.
:param str _id: Unique delegated permission identifier inside the collection of delegated permissions defined
    for a resource application.
:param str is_enabled: When creating or updating a permission, this property must be set to true
    (which is the default). To delete a permission, this property must first be set to false.
    At that point, in a subsequent call, the permission may be removed.

Represents the definition of a delegated permission.

Delegated permissions can be requested by client applications needing an access token to the API which defined the
permissions. Delegated permissions can be requested dynamically, using the scopes parameter in an authorization
request to the Microsoft identity platform, or statically, through the requiredResourceAccess collection on the
application object.

classes.name: office365.directory.certificates.self_signed.SelfSignedCertificate

methods.name: office365.directory.certificates.self_signed.SelfSignedCertificate.__init__
documentation: :param str display_name:
Contains the public part of a signing certificate.

classes.name: office365.directory.certificates.authority.CertificateAuthority

methods.name: office365.directory.certificates.authority.CertificateAuthority.__init__
documentation:
:param str certificate: The base64 encoded string representing the public certificate.
:param str certificate_revocation_list_url: The URL of the certificate revocation list.
:param str is_root_authority: Required. true if the trusted certificate is a root authority, false if the
    trusted certificate is an intermediate authority.
:param str issuer: The issuer of the certificate, calculated from the certificate value
:param str issuer_ski: The subject key identifier of the certificate, calculated from the certificate value.
Represents a certificate authority.

classes.name: office365.directory.certificates.certification.Certification

methods.name: office365.directory.certificates.certification.Certification.__init__
documentation: :param str certification_details_url:
Represents the certification details of an application.

classes.name: office365.directory.custom_security_attribute_definition.CustomSecurityAttributeDefinition
documentation:
Represents the schema of a custom security attribute (key-value pair).
For example, the custom security attribute name, description, data type, and allowed values.

classes.name: office365.directory.datapolicy.operation.DataPolicyOperation
documentation:
Represents a submitted data policy operation. It contains necessary information for tracking the status of
an operation. For example, a company administrator can submit a data policy operation request to export an
employee's company data, and then later track that request.

classes.name: office365.directory.tenant_relationship.TenantRelationship

methods.name: office365.directory.tenant_relationship.TenantRelationship.find_tenant_information_by_domain_name
documentation:
Given a domain name, search for a tenant and read its tenantInformation. You can use this API to
validate tenant information and use their tenantId to configure cross-tenant access settings between you
and the tenant.

:param str domain_name: Primary domain name of an Azure AD tenant.
Represent the various type of tenant relationships.

classes.name: office365.directory.identities.conditional_access_root.ConditionalAccessRoot

methods.name: office365.directory.identities.conditional_access_root.ConditionalAccessRoot.authentication_strength
documentation: The entry point for the Conditional Access (CA) object model.

The conditionalAccessRoot resource is the entry point for the Conditional Access (CA) object model.
It doesn't contain any usable properties.

classes.name: office365.directory.identities.api_connector.IdentityApiConnector

methods.name: office365.directory.identities.api_connector.IdentityApiConnector.upload_client_certificate
documentation:
Upload a PKCS 12 format key (.pfx) to an API connector's authentication configuration.
The input is a base-64 encoded value of the PKCS 12 certificate contents.
This method returns an apiConnector.

:param str pkcs12_value:
:param str password:

methods.name: office365.directory.identities.api_connector.IdentityApiConnector.authentication_configuration
documentation:
The object which describes the authentication configuration details for calling the API.
Basic and PKCS 12 client certificate are supported.

Represents API connectors in an Azure Active Directory (Azure AD) tenants.

An API connector used in your Azure AD External Identities self-service sign-up user flows allows you to call
an API during the execution of the user flow. An API connector provides the information needed to call an API
including an endpoint URL and authentication. An API connector can be used at a specific step in a user flow
to affect the execution of the user flow. For example, the API response can block a user from signing up,
show an input validation error, or overwrite user collected attributes.

methods.name: office365.directory.identities.provider_collection.IdentityProviderCollection.available_provider_types
documentation:
Get all identity providers supported in a directory.

The identityProvider API is deprecated and will stop returning data on March 2023.
Please use the new identityProviderBase API.

classes.name: office365.directory.identities.object_identity.ObjectIdentity

methods.name: office365.directory.identities.object_identity.ObjectIdentity.__init__
documentation:
:param str sign_in_type: Specifies the user sign-in types in your directory, such as emailAddress, userName
    or federated. Here, federated represents a unique identifier for a user from an issuer, that can be in
    any format chosen by the issuer. Additional validation is enforced on issuerAssignedId when the sign-in
    type is set to emailAddress or userName. This property can also be set to any custom string.
:param str issuer: Specifies the issuer of the identity, for example facebook.com.
    For local accounts (where signInType is not federated), this property is the local B2C tenant default
    domain name, for example contoso.onmicrosoft.com.
    For external users from other Azure AD organization, this will be the domain of the federated organization,
    for example contoso.com.
:param str issuer_assigned_id: Specifies the unique identifier assigned to the user by the issuer.
    The combination of issuer and issuerAssignedId must be unique within the organization. Represents
    the sign-in name for the user, when signInType is set to emailAddress or userName
    (also known as local accounts).
Represents an identity used to sign in to a user account.

classes.name: office365.directory.identities.userflows.attribute.IdentityUserFlowAttribute
documentation:
Represents user flow attributes in an Azure Active Directory (Azure AD) tenant.

Configuring user flow attributes in your Azure AD tenant allows you to collect information about
a user during sign-up. You can choose to collect a built-in set of attributes. For example, given name, surname,
city, and postal code. You can also configure custom user flow attributes to collect information
from a user that is not built in to the directory. Custom user flow attributes are an abstraction over
Azure Active Directory schema extensions.

classes.name: office365.directory.identities.userflows.language_page.UserFlowLanguagePage
documentation:
Determines the user flow language pages that are shown to users during a user flow.
These language pages include both the default language translations provided by Microsoft,
or custom pages that can be created to customize the language translations.

documentation: Collection of pages with the default content to display in a user flow for a specified language.

Allows a user flow to support the use of multiple languages.

For Azure Active Directory user flows, you can only leverage the built-in languages provided by Microsoft.
User flows for Azure Active Directory support defining the language and strings shown to users
as they go through the journeys you configure with your user flows.

User flows are used to enable a self-service sign up experience for guest users on an application.
User flows define the experience the end user sees while signing up, including which identity providers they can
use to authenticate, along with which attributes are collected as part of the sign up process.

classes.name: office365.directory.identitygovernance.accessreview.scope.AccessReviewScope
documentation:
The accessReviewScope defines what entities are reviewed in an accessReviewScheduleDefinition. It's an abstract type
that is inherited by accessReviewQueryScope, principalResourceMembershipsScope, and accessReviewReviewerScope.

For scope property on an accessReviewScheduleDefinition see accessReviewQueryScope and
principalResourceMembershipsScope.

For reviewers property on an accessReviewScheduleDefinition see accessReviewReviewerScope.

Specifying the OData type in the scope is highly recommended for all types but required for
principalResourceMembershipsScope and accessReviewInactiveUserQueryScope.

    See known limitations for those combinations of two or more entity types that are supported in the
    same search request.
:param list[str] fields: Contains the fields to be returned for each resource object specified in entityTypes,
    allowing customization of the fields returned by default; otherwise, including additional fields such
    as custom managed properties from SharePoint and OneDrive, or custom fields in externalItem from the
    content that Microsoft Graph connectors bring in. The fields property can use the semantic labels
    applied to properties. For example, if a property is labeled as title, you can retrieve it using
    the following syntax: label_title.

documentation: Gets a value that specifies the identifier for the list template.

Represents a list definition or a list template, which defines the fields and views for a list.
List definitions are contained in files within
\Program Files\Common Files\Microsoft Shared\Web Server Extensions\12\TEMPLATE\FEATURES,
but list templates are created through the user interface or through the object model when a list is
saved as a template.
Use the Web.ListTemplates property (section 3.2.5.143.1.2.13) to return a ListTemplateCollection
(section 3.2.5.92) for a site collection. Use an indexer to return a single list definition or
list template from the collection.

classes.name: office365.sharepoint.contenttypes.content_type_id.ContentTypeId

methods.name: office365.sharepoint.contenttypes.content_type_id.ContentTypeId.__init__
documentation:
Represents the content type identifier (ID) of a content type.

:param str string_value: Hexadecimal string value of content type identifier. String value MUST start with "0x".

The ContentTypeId type is the identifier for the specified content type. The identifier is a string of
hexadecimal characters. The identifier MUST be unique relative to the current site collection and site and MUST
follow the pattern of prefixing a ContentTypeId with its parent ContentTypeId.

ContentTypeId MUST follow the XSD pattern specified in [MS-WSSCAML] section 2.3.1.4.

Specifies a content type.

A named and uniquely identifiable collection of settings and fields that store metadata for individual items
in a SharePoint list. One or more content types can be associated with a list, which restricts the contents
to items of those types

    If the collection is empty, no update will take place.

    A value of "true" means yes.

methods.name: office365.sharepoint.lists.list.List.save_as_new_view
documentation:
Overwrites a view if it already exists, creates a new view if it does not; and then extracts the
implementation-specific filter and sort information from the URL and builds and updates the view's XML.
Returns the URL of the view.

:param str old_name: The name of the view the user is currently on.
:param str new_name: The new name given by the user.
:param bool private_view: Set to "true" to make the view private; otherwise, "false".
:param str uri: URL that contains all the implementation-specific filter and sort information for the view.

methods.name: office365.sharepoint.lists.list.List.save_as_template
documentation:
Saves the list as a template in the list template gallery and includes the option of saving with or
without the data that is contained in the current list.

:param bool save_data: true to save the data of the original list along with the list template; otherwise, false
:param str description: A string that contains the description for the list template.
:param str name: A string that contains the title for the list template.
:param str file_name: A string that contains the file name for the list template with an .stp extension.

methods.name: office365.sharepoint.lists.list.List.add_item_using_path
documentation:
Adds a ListItem to an existing List.

:param str leaf_name: Specifies the name of the list item that will be created. In the case of a
    document library, the name is equal to the filename of the list item.
:param int object_type: Specifies the file system object type for the item that will be created.
    It MUST be either FileSystemObjectType.File or FileSystemObjectType.Folder.
:param str ot None folder_url: Specifies the url of the folder of the new list item.
    The value MUST be either null or the decoded url value an empty string or a server-relative
    URL or an absolute URL. If the value is not null or the decoded url value not being empty string,
    the decoded url value MUST point to a location within the list.

methods.name: office365.sharepoint.lists.list.List.add_validate_update_item
documentation:
Adds an item to an existing list and validate the list item update values. If all fields validated successfully,
 commit all changes. If there's any exception in any of the fields, the item will not be committed.

:param ListItemCreationInformation create_info:  Contains the information that determines how the item
    will be created.
:param dict form_values: A collection of field internal names and values for the given field. If the collection
    is empty, no update will take place.

documentation: Specifies whether the list supports content types.

methods.name: office365.sharepoint.lists.list.List.allow_deletion

documentation:
Specifies the base type of the list.
It MUST be one of the following values: GenericList, DocumentLibrary, DiscussionBoard, Survey, or Issue.

methods.name: office365.sharepoint.lists.list.List.browser_file_handling
documentation:
Specifies the override at the list level for the BrowserFileHandling property of the Web application.
If the BrowserFileHandling property of the Web application is BrowserFileHandling.Strict,
then this setting MUST be ignored. If the BrowserFileHandling property of the Web application is
BrowserFileHandling.Permissive, then this setting MUST be respected.

methods.name: office365.sharepoint.lists.list.List.created
documentation: Specifies when the list was created.

methods.name: office365.sharepoint.lists.list.List.default_display_form_url
documentation: Specifies the location of the default display form for the list.

methods.name: office365.sharepoint.lists.list.List.default_view_path
documentation: Specifies the server-relative URL of the default view for the list.

methods.name: office365.sharepoint.lists.list.List.default_view_url
documentation: Specifies the server-relative URL of the default view for the list.

methods.name: office365.sharepoint.lists.list.List.crawl_non_default_views
documentation:
Specifies whether or not the crawler indexes the non-default views of the list.
Specify a value of true if the crawler indexes the list's non-default views; specify false if otherwise.

methods.name: office365.sharepoint.lists.list.List.creatables_info
documentation:
Returns an object that describes what this list can create, and a collection of links to visit in order to
create those things. If it can't create certain things, it contains an error message describing why.

 The consumer MUST append the encoded URL of the current page to the links returned here.
 (This page the link goes to needs it as a query parameter to function correctly.)
 The consumer SHOULD also consider appending &IsDlg=1 to the link, to remove the UI from the linked page,
 if desired.

methods.name: office365.sharepoint.lists.list.List.current_change_token
documentation: Gets the current change token that is used in the change log for the list.

methods.name: office365.sharepoint.lists.list.List.data_source
documentation:
Specifies the data source of an external list.
If HasExternalDataSource is "false", the server MUST return NULL.

methods.name: office365.sharepoint.lists.list.List.default_edit_form_url
documentation: Gets a value that specifies the URL of the edit form to use for list items in the list.

methods.name: office365.sharepoint.lists.list.List.default_item_open_use_list_setting
documentation:
This property returns whether to use the List setting or the server wide setting for
DefaultItemOpen (BrowserEnabledDocuments setting) in the Web application. This property can only be set to
false, thereby using the server-wide setting. To set it to use List setting, set the value for DefaultItemOpen.

methods.name: office365.sharepoint.lists.list.List.document_template_url
documentation: Specifies the URL of the document template assigned to the list.

methods.name: office365.sharepoint.lists.list.List.effective_base_permissions
documentation: Specifies the effective permissions on the list that are assigned to the current user.

methods.name: office365.sharepoint.lists.list.List.effective_base_permissions_for_ui
documentation:
Specifies the effective base permissions for the current user as they SHOULD be displayed in user interface (UI)
If the list is not in read-only UI mode, the value of EffectiveBasePermissionsForUI MUST be the same as the
value of EffectiveBasePermissions (section 3.2.5.79.1.1.17). If the list is in read-only UI mode, the value
of EffectiveBasePermissionsForUI MUST be a subset of the value of EffectiveBasePermissions.

methods.name: office365.sharepoint.lists.list.List.enable_assign_to_email
documentation: Gets or sets a Boolean value specifying whether e-mail notification is enabled for the list.

methods.name: office365.sharepoint.lists.list.List.enable_attachments
documentation: Specifies whether list item attachments are enabled for the list

methods.name: office365.sharepoint.lists.list.List.enable_folder_creation
documentation: Specifies whether new list folders can be added to the list.

methods.name: office365.sharepoint.lists.list.List.enable_minor_versions
